role_summary: Civil Rights History Project interviewee; co-subject of a joint oral history with Embry Howell (catalog entry 67).
ai_reading: Joseph Howell shares the embedding signature of the joint oral history at entry 67 (catalog slug 'joseph-howell-and-embry-howell'); see that catalog page for the substrate-derived neighbor cluster and axis observations the embedding produces for the combined transcript. Individual cosines for each named subject of a joint interview are not separable from the precomputed substrate, since the LoC transcript was recorded as a single audio file with multiple voices interleaved.
biographical_paragraph: Joseph Howell is one of the named subjects of the joint Civil Rights History Project oral history at entry 67 (American Folklife Center accession AFC 2010/039) [src: 1]. This catalog page exists as a per-person index for Joseph Howell; the substrate signature lives on the parent joint-interview page (see the AI's reading above) since the LoC transcript treats the joint conversation as a single recording. Independent biographical sourcing for Joseph Howell is pending.

sources.title: Joint oral history at entry 67
sources.publisher: Civil Rights History Project, Library of Congress (AFC 2010/039)

gallery.alt: Chambers Building, Davidson College, Davidson, North Carolina
gallery.caption: The Chambers Building at Davidson College in North Carolina, where Joseph Howell was a student when he organized a 1964 civil rights march in Charlotte, before working for SNCC in Southwest Georgia in 1966.
